channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Apache chef Nephi Craig says cooking Native food saved his life
item.description: Craig has cooked in a high-end restaurant — and for 7,800 prisoners in jail. He writes about cooking, his struggle with addition and his Native American heritage in the memoir Our Knives Will Save Us.
item.content:encoded:
Craig has cooked in a high-end restaurant — and for 7,800 prisoners in jail. He writes about cooking, his struggle with addition and his Native American heritage in the memoir Our Knives Will Save Us.

(Image credit: Ari Carter Craig)
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: An eco-journalist takes on a Big Tech in this modern twist on the heist novel
item.description: Nathaniel Rich's literary thriller centers on a young couple who strike out against a data center. Cloudthief wraps a smart exploration of our data-dominated society inside an entertaining heist yarn.
item.content:encoded: Nathaniel Rich's literary thriller centers on a young couple who strike out against a data center. Cloudthief wraps a smart exploration of our data-dominated society inside an entertaining heist yarn.
item.dc:creator: John Powers

item.title: Thrilling but uneven, 'The Odyssey' is a homecoming for Christopher Nolan
item.description: The Odyssey feels like a prism through which Nolan's earlier films can be understood. A downbeat realist by nature, Nolan imbues the epic with a fascinating tension between fantasy and skepticism.
item.content:encoded:
The Odyssey feels like a prism through which Nolan's earlier films can be understood. A downbeat realist by nature, Nolan imbues the epic with a fascinating tension between fantasy and skepticism.

(Image credit: Melinda Sue Gordon)
item.dc:creator: Justin Chang

item.title: Trump wants a permanent fence around the D.C. park known for White House protests
item.description: The Trump administration wants to install permanent fencing around Lafayette Park, directly outside the White House. It's long been a popular spot for protesters, who worry barriers will change that.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration wants to install permanent fencing around Lafayette Park, directly outside the White House. It's long been a popular spot for protesters, who worry barriers will change that.

(Image credit: U.S. Commission of Fine Arts)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: 10 photographers share favorite moments from the Tiny Desk Contest Tour
item.description: Ten photographers share their favorite memories from the 2026 Tiny Desk Contest On The Road tour, which was headlined by this year's winner, the Dallas hip-hop group Cure for Paranoia.
item.content:encoded:
Ten photographers share their favorite memories from the 2026 Tiny Desk Contest On The Road tour, which was headlined by this year's winner, the Dallas hip-hop group Cure for Paranoia.

(Image credit: Jovelle Tamayo for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Elle Mannion

item.title: 'Gossip' had godly origins. Here's how it gained its sinful reputation
item.description: We've all engaged in a little 'gossip.' But did you know its origins were tied to family and friendship?
item.content:encoded:
We've all engaged in a little 'gossip.' But did you know its origins were tied to family and friendship?

(Image credit: Fox Photos/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Jaclyn Diaz

item.title: Old-school digicams are everywhere. Why?
item.description: People who weren't yet alive in the 1990s and early 2000s are buying up old-school point-and-shoot cameras. For some, it's a trendy retro vibe; for others, a rebellion against the smartphone era.
item.content:encoded: People who weren't yet alive in the 1990s and early 2000s are buying up old-school point-and-shoot cameras. For some, it's a trendy retro vibe; for others, a rebellion against the smartphone era.
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh

item.title: Trump relished in being compared to dictators like Hitler and Stalin, journalist says
item.description: The New York Times journalist Jonathan Swan says the president is fixated on becoming a "great man of history" during his second term. Swan's new book, written with Maggie Haberman, is Regime Change.
item.content:encoded:
The New York Times journalist Jonathan Swan says the president is fixated on becoming a "great man of history" during his second term. Swan's new book, written with Maggie Haberman, is Regime Change.

(Image credit: Jacquelyn Martin)
item.dc:creator: Dave Davies

item.title: What do a bar and a Muslim community organization have in common?
item.description: Artists create, even during times of chaos. In response to Trump policies and ICE's incursions, arts organizations are leaning in to new ways of fostering community in ways large and small.
item.content:encoded:
Artists create, even during times of chaos. In response to Trump policies and ICE's incursions, arts organizations are leaning in to new ways of fostering community in ways large and small.

(Image credit: Olivia Obineme for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: Too weak to advocate for herself, a young woman is saved by a nun
item.description: When Solitaire Miles was 18, she had a stroke. When she got to the hospital, doctors and nurses accused her of being on drugs. Then, her unsung hero arrived and ensured she got the care she needed.
item.content:encoded:
When Solitaire Miles was 18, she had a stroke. When she got to the hospital, doctors and nurses accused her of being on drugs. Then, her unsung hero arrived and ensured she got the care she needed.

(Image credit: Solitaire Miles)
item.dc:creator: Autumn Barnes
